version: MITRE ATLAS — curated LLM-relevant excerpts (as of 2026)

entries.name: LLM Prompt Injection
entries.description: An adversary crafts inputs to an LLM-integrated application that override or bypass the system prompt or its operating policy. Direct prompt injection is supplied by the user; indirect prompt injection is carried in content retrieved from external sources (web pages, documents, emails, alert payloads).

entries.name: LLM Jailbreak
entries.description: An adversary uses adversarial prompting techniques to bypass alignment guardrails of a deployed LLM, eliciting outputs the model was trained or configured to refuse.

entries.name: LLM Data Leakage
entries.description: An LLM emits sensitive information from its context window, retrieved documents, or training data — typically via prompt injection or carefully crafted queries.

entries.name: LLM Plugin / Tool Compromise
entries.description: An attacker abuses tool-calling or plugin APIs that the LLM is wired to so it performs unintended actions (deleting data, sending messages, calling internal APIs).

entries.name: Full ML Model Access
entries.description: An adversary has unrestricted query-level or white-box access to the model, enabling staged adversarial example generation and membership-inference attacks.

entries.name: ML Model Inference API Access
entries.description: Adversary queries a model only through an inference API. Volumes of queries can reveal decision boundaries or enable evasion crafting.

entries.name: Adversarial Input Detection
entries.description: Deploy detectors for known adversarial-input patterns (prompt-injection signatures, anomalous token distributions, suspicious encodings) at the input boundary before the model is invoked.

entries.name: Model Output Filtering
entries.description: Filter and constrain model outputs (regex, classifiers, content filters, schema validation) before they are returned to a user or passed to a downstream tool.

entries.name: Sanitize Training Data
entries.description: Remove PII, credentials, and adversarial samples from training and fine-tuning data. Maintain provenance records for what was included.

entries.name: Constrain Tool Permissions
entries.description: Apply least-privilege to agent tools. Require human approval for irreversible actions. Segment tool access by data sensitivity.

entries.name: Restrict Number of ML Model Queries
entries.description: Rate-limit per-principal model queries to slow staged adversarial attacks and detect anomalous volume.
